tldr: Codemagic and DeployHQ solve different stages: Codemagic builds and tests mobile apps, DeployHQ pushes built code to servers via Git/SSH/SFTP. A direct switch only makes sense if your real need is Git-to-server deploys, not mobile builds.
why_switch: Simpler if you only need deploy-on-push, not full CI
Standard Git/SSH/SFTP deploys with minimal lock-in
what_to_export: Build artifacts you want to keep
export_method: Codemagic workflows live as codemagic.yaml in your repo or as UI configuration; export both. Use the Codemagic web UI to list environment groups and document variable names; secret values themselves should be pulled from your password manager because they cannot be read back. Export signing material from your secure store rather than from Codemagic. Note that DeployHQ does not run mobile builds, so any iOS/Android compilation steps will need to remain on a build system; you will only migrate the deploy portion of your pipeline. Capture a list of deploy targets (servers, paths, protocols, credentials) that you currently configure inside Codemagic post-build steps.
import_method: In DeployHQ, create a project per repository and connect the Git source. Configure each server as a deployment target with the appropriate protocol (SFTP, SSH, or supported cloud target) and credentials. Define the build commands DeployHQ should run (DeployHQ supports running build commands as part of the deploy). For mobile binaries, keep your build pipeline on a CI system that can produce signed artifacts, then have that pipeline upload the artifact and trigger a DeployHQ deployment via webhook or API. Set up branch-to-server mapping, deployment notifications, and team access. Run a test deploy to a staging server, validate file ownership and permissions, then point production at DeployHQ.
effort: 1-2 weeks if you only need server deploys; longer if you also need to relocate mobile builds elsewhere.
gotchas: DeployHQ is not a mobile build service; you still need a builder for iOS/Android
App Store/Play Store publishing flows are not native to DeployHQ
Recreate signing securely; never paste keystores into deploy logs
Permissions on deployed files often differ between SFTP servers
Webhook-driven cross-tool flow needs careful retry/idempotency design
Plan rollback strategy; DeployHQ rollbacks differ from Codemagic reruns

email.subject: Moving deploy automation to DeployHQ, closing Codemagic account
email.body:
Hi Codemagic team,

Our deployment needs have shifted toward straightforward Git-to-server pushes rather than full mobile build pipelines, and we are moving that workflow to DeployHQ. We want to wrap up our Codemagic usage cleanly over the next 14 days.

The plan: stand up DeployHQ projects this week, dual-run with Codemagic for about a week to validate, then stop new builds on Codemagic and request account closure. We would appreciate one extra billing cycle of read-only access so we can pull any logs or artifacts we still need.

Could you confirm:
- How to formally cancel and the final billing date
- Whether build artifacts remain downloadable after cancellation
- Any export tooling you recommend before closure

Thanks for the help over the years. The product worked well; this is a scope change on our side.

Your customer
